I'm a 16-year-old full-stack developer from Pune who started coding at 10 and hasn't really stopped. I build things that solve real problems — or at least problems I find interesting. Still learning, always shipping.

scroll
01 — About Me

A teenager who codes, skates, and watches way too much anime.

I'm Arnav Rana — also known online as Hacker789 or GlitchHavoc — a 16-year-old developer based in Pune, Maharashtra. Born on October 25, 2009, in a household where curiosity was always encouraged. I fell into coding around age 10, starting with HTML and CSS, and the rabbit hole never ended.

Coding for me was never about being "a programmer." It was about building things that actually work. Watchtopia — a streaming platform I built from scratch — taught me more about systems design and database architecture than any tutorial could. PhotoBeats, my current project, came from a simple frustration: making a photo edit to a song manually is tedious. So I'm automating it with AI.

Right now I'm a Class 12 student at Vedantu Aundh, Pune, preparing for JEE while somehow also managing side projects, cricket matches, and a Pokémon card collection. I've binged 80+ anime and 30+ web series (Stranger Things is a religion). I love physics, mythology books, thrillers, and I have very strong opinions about White Monster being superior.

I'm honest about where I am. I'm not a senior engineer with 10 years of experience. I'm a kid who builds things, breaks them, fixes them, and ships them. If you're looking for someone with genuine curiosity and the drive to figure things out — I'm your guy.

Currently building:

PhotoBeats · AI-powered photo-music sync editor

02 — Learning Journey

How I got here.

2016 · Age 7
The Skating Years
Won silver medals at LazerXtech Open and Fighter Skate PDRAS. Bronze at Ace Rollers. This was my prime.
2019 · Age 10
First Lines of Code
Discovered HTML and CSS. Built my first "website" — a garish page with every background color I could find.
2022 · Age 13
JavaScript & Java
Started learning logic-based programming. OOP clicked with Java. JavaScript made the web feel alive.
2024 · Age 15
Watchtopia Ships
Built and launched my first real full-stack project. Learned database design, streaming infra, and server architecture the hard way.
2025–26 · Age 16
PhotoBeats + AI/ML
Learning Python, TensorFlow, audio analysis. Building PhotoBeats while juggling JEE prep. Current chapter.

04 — Other Projects

Things I've shipped.

Watchtopia

A fully functional free streaming platform I built from scratch. Handles video delivery, user sessions, and a catalogue interface. My first real full-stack challenge — I learned what "database design" actually means the hard way.

HTML/CSS/JS Full-Stack DB Design
● Live Visit Site
GOAT — God Tier Tools

A hub of 60+ free browser-based utilities — PDF merge/split/compress, background remover, OCR, screen recorder, image converter, JSON viewer, M3U8 downloader, and more. No installs, no signup, everything runs client-side.

Web Tools JavaScript Browser-Based
● Live Visit Site
MJ Hub

A tribute to Michael Jackson built as a collection of interactive mini-apps — a music player, a moonwalk side-scroller, pinball, mafia, a memory game, a reaction-speed tester, a color palette maker, and more, all wired together through one game bar hub. Built for the Outlier AI Hackathon.

React Vite Tailwind
● Live Visit Site

Every car. Every memory.
